question: According to the USGS, in what year was the American Alligator first found west of Texas (not including Texas)?

chat_for_question.content:
=== QUESTION (GAIA Level 2) ===
According to the USGS, in what year was the American Alligator first found west of Texas (not including Texas)?

Call budget for this question: 1 (this call).

Respond with:
<final_answer>Your answer</final_answer>

To find the year the American Alligator was first found west of Texas (excluding Texas itself) according to the USGS, we refer to the **USGS Nonindigenous Aquatic Species (NAS)** database.

The USGS NAS fact sheet for *Alligator mississippiensis* (American Alligator) contains some contradictory summary text, but the data records are clear. The summary section of the fact sheet states that the first record west of Texas was from California in 1977. However, the same fact sheet, under the "New Mexico" state-by-state section, explicitly identifies a much earlier record: "The first record of Alligator mississippiensis in New Mexico was a specimen collected from the Rio Grande near Las Cruces in **1892** (Cope 1900)."

This 1892 record (Specimen ID 161830) is the earliest occurrence of the species listed by the USGS in any state west of Texas. Although the species is native to the southeastern United States (as far west as the Rio Grande in Texas), the USGS considers the 1892 New Mexico finding to be "well outside the species' native range" and thus a non-indigenous occurrence.

<final_answer>1892</final_answer>
